evidence:
CollocateProfileComparator has 12 consecutive logger.debug() calls including banner headers: '
=== SEMANTIC FIELD COMPARISON ===', '----...----' separator lines, section headers '
--- Building Comparison Profiles ---', '
Top SHARED (high commonality):', '
Top DISTINCTIVE (specific to 1 noun):' — this is noisy trace-style debug logging that reads like a development console session left in production code

SketchHandlers.handleConcordanceExamples has 4 logger.debug() calls logging intermediate pattern transformation steps (patternWithHead, collocatePosition, bcqlQuery, and relation-not-found); these are implementation trace logs, not diagnostic signals
SketchHandlers.handleVisualRadial has 2 logger.debug() calls: 'Radial: body = {}' and 'Radial: center = {}' — restating inputs at entry, no diagnostic value

SemanticFieldExplorer has 8+ logger.debug() calls tracing every step: 'Exploring semantic field: seed=...', '  Total candidate nouns: ...', '  Nouns with X+ shared: ...', '  No results found for seed word. Trying fallback...' — these are development-era step-by-step traces

ExplorationHandlers.handleSemanticFieldExplore and handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti each have logger.info('Received request: {}', exchange.getRequestURI()) at the top — this exists in only 2 of the 4 exploration handlers and is absent from the sketch handlers; inconsistent entry logging with no observable pattern
No restating comments that echo the code — comments explain WHY, not WHAT; this is clean
No defensive null checks on typed non-nullable values (records with non-null components are trusted)
No multi-line docstrings on trivial 2-line functions — Javadoc is proportional to method complexity
No pass-through wrapper functions beyond the intentional SemanticFieldExplorer.compareCollocateProfiles facade (which is documented)
Domain names are used consistently in domain code — no handleData, processItem patterns found

CollocateQueryHelper.scoreHits has a comment 'safe: min ensures result <= maxResults*10' where 10 is the hard-coded value of OVER_FETCH_FACTOR in the comment — the constant itself is 3, not 10; this is a stale comment from when the constant was a different value

strengths: Comments throughout the codebase explain non-obvious decisions (e.g., why ExplorationResult is a class not a record, why ExploreOptions uses 'Explore' not 'Exploration', OVER_FETCH_FACTOR rationale)
No generic domain names (handleData, processItem) in production code
Javadoc is present only where it adds value — trivial accessors are undocumented
ai_generated_debt.issue_character: Issues are concentrated in verbose entry/exit debug logging in CollocateProfileComparator and SemanticFieldExplorer that traces every algorithm step and uses ASCII banner separators — a hallmark of LLM-assisted development sessions where debug output was left in rather than converted to meaningful diagnostic logging.
ai_generated_debt.score_rationale: The codebase has clean comments and appropriate Javadoc. However, the exploration subsystem carries 20+ trace-level debug statements including ASCII banners and section headers that are the signature of iterative LLM-assisted development. Two handler methods also log request entry inconsistently. A 62 reflects meaningful technical debt in logging verbosity that would need a cleanup pass before the codebase is fully professional.

issues.summary: CollocateProfileComparator uses ASCII banner debug logging with separator lines and section headers — development-era trace output left in production code
evidence: logger.debug("\n=== SEMANTIC FIELD COMPARISON ===")
logger.debug("\n--- Building Comparison Profiles ---")
logger.debug("\nTop SHARED (high commonality):")
logger.debug("\nTop DISTINCTIVE (specific to 1 noun):")
12 consecutive debug statements tracing every phase of the algorithm — more console session than structured logging
issues.suggestion: Remove ASCII banner/separator debug lines entirely. Replace step-count logs ('Found {} adjectives', 'Total unique adjectives: {}') with a single structured summary log at the end of compareCollocateProfiles: logger.debug('Comparison complete: {} nouns, {} adjectives found', nounList.size(), profiles.size()). The per-noun detail logs ('Profiling: {}', 'Top 5: {}') should be removed or guarded behind a trace flag.

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer has 8+ debug statements tracing every exploration algorithm step — development-era trace output
evidence: logger.debug("Exploring semantic field: seed='{}', relation='{}', top={}, minShared={}, minLogDice={}", ...)
logger.debug("  Total candidate nouns: {}", nounProfiles.size())
logger.debug("  Nouns with {}+ shared: {}", minShared, discoveredNouns.size())
logger.debug("  {}: {} nouns", collocate, nouns.size()) — this is inside a per-collocate loop, emitting one log line per collocate
logger.debug("Exploration complete for '{}': {} nouns discovered, {} core collocates", ...)
issues.suggestion: Consolidate to two log statements: one at method entry with the key parameters, and one at exit with the result counts. Remove the per-collocate loop log entirely (it can emit hundreds of lines per request). The fallback log ('Trying fallback to simple pattern') is useful and should be kept at WARN level.

issues.summary: handleConcordanceExamples logs 4 intermediate pattern transformation steps at DEBUG level — development trace left in handler code
evidence: logger.debug("After getFullPattern: {}", patternWithHead)
logger.debug("collocatePosition = {}", rel.get().collocatePosition())
logger.debug("After substituteCollocate: {}", bcqlQuery)
logger.debug("Relation '{}' not found in grammar config", relation) — this is WARN-level information (a potentially unexpected state), not DEBUG
issues.suggestion: Remove the three step-trace debug logs (getFullPattern, collocatePosition, substituteCollocate). Promote the 'Relation not found' log to WARN level since it indicates a misconfiguration or unexpected client input.

issues.summary: logger.info('Received request: {}') appears in 2 of 4 exploration handler methods but is absent from all sketch handler methods
evidence: WordSketchApiServer.registerRoutes: lambda for explore and explore-multi both call logger.info('Received request: {}', exchange.getRequestURI()) before invoking the handler
No equivalent log exists in any SketchHandlers method or in handleSemanticFieldComparison, handleSemanticFieldExamples
The logs were added at the call site (in lambdas in WordSketchApiServer) rather than inside the handler methods — inconsistent placement
issues.suggestion: Either add request logging uniformly in wrapHandler (one place, all endpoints) or remove the two ad-hoc logger.info calls in the lambda sites. The wrapHandler approach is preferable as it ensures consistent coverage.

issues.summary: Comment in CollocateQueryHelper.scoreHits references 'maxResults*10' but the actual OVER_FETCH_FACTOR constant is 3
evidence: int limit = Math.min(maxResults * OVER_FETCH_FACTOR, records.size()); // safe: min ensures result <= maxResults*10
OVER_FETCH_FACTOR = 3, not 10 — the comment says *10 which is incorrect
issues.suggestion: Update the comment to 'safe: min ensures result <= maxResults * OVER_FETCH_FACTOR' or remove the comment (the Math.min is self-explanatory).

root_causes: Exploration algorithm developed iteratively with debug logging as the primary observability tool; logging was never cleaned up after development stabilized
Entry logging added ad-hoc to two specific endpoints during debugging and never made uniform
likely_symptoms: debug log volume making it difficult to diagnose real issues in exploration subsystem
